jules verne.example_clue: "Captain Nemo looked at me. I asked him no questions, but he guessed my thoughts"

jane austen.example_clue: "'What a pity it is, Elinor,' said Marianne, 'that Edward should have no taste for drawing'"

hunter s. thompson.example_clue: "The sporting editors had also given me $300 in cash, most of which was already spent on extremely dangerous drugs"

joyce.example_clue: "Ask again yes and then he asked me would I yes to say yes my mountain flower and first I put my arms around him yes"

(mary) shelley.example_clue: "Your threats... confirm me in a determination of not creating you a companion in vice. Shall I... set loose... a daemon"?

world war i.example_clue: "A Farewell to Arms" is based on Ernest Hemingway's experiences in this conflict

the natural.example_clue: Brooklyn Dodgers fan Bernard Malamud gained fame with this debut novel about a baseball phenom

mcmurtry.example_clue: The 1986 Pulitzer Prize for Fiction went to this author's "Lonesome Dove"

sinclair lewis.example_clue: He won a Nobel Prize for Literature in 1930 shortly after his novel "Dodsworth" had been published

emerson.example_clue: His 1841 essay "Self-Reliance" urges the reader not to be ruled by society's regulations or traditions

the da vinci code.example_clue: This Dan Brown novel contains a poem that begins, "The Holy Grail 'neath ancient Roslin waits"

through the looking-glass.example_clue: "Jabberwocky" is a nonsense poem found in this sequel from 1871

gloucester.example_clue: In a Kipling novel Old Captain Edwardes reads a poem at the Memorial Day of this Massachusetts fishing town

doctor zhivago.example_clue: This Pasternak epic contains dozens of poems, including "Indian Summer" & "Hops"

tennyson.example_clue: Chapters in Meg Cabot's "Avalon High" begin with lines from this poet's "Lady of Shalott"

the age of innocence.example_clue: Roxane Gay's go-to book is this Pulitzer Prize winner by Edith Wharton set in Old New York

the great gatsby.example_clue: Jay McInerney called this "love story of a bootlegger & a southern belle" "the Rosetta Stone of the American dream"

madeline.example_clue: Judy Blume said this 1939 work set at an all-girl boarding school in Paris was the first book she fell in love with

the remains of the day.example_clue: Filmmaker Emerald Fennell loves the British restraint of this 1989 novel by Kazuo Ishiguro: "A perfect story of lost love & regret"

fanny price.example_clue: Alison Bechdel of "Fun Home" fame loves "Mansfield Park" & identifies with this lead character

alice.example_clue: As she's falling down a hole in an 1865 work, she dreams of walking "hand in hand" with her cat Dinah; it gives one paws...

chandler.example_clue: In "The Long Goodbye" by this author, P.I. Philip Marlowe feels "as hollow & empty as the spaces between the stars"

angelou.example_clue: In an autobiography by this poet who passed in 2014, she calls William Shakespeare her "first white love"

murakami.example_clue: In "1Q84" by this Japanese author, Aomame sees "two moons... clearly floating in the sky"

(elena) ferrante.example_clue: Near the end of a work by this author, Lila tells her pal, "You're my brilliant friend, you have to be the best of all, boys and girls"

the scarlet letter.example_clue: In "Easy A" Emma Stone gets high marks in an adaptation of this study in morals from 1850

stand by me.example_clue: The Stephen King novella "The Body" became this movie, also a better choice for a song title

hellraiser.example_clue: Any Pinhead might know Clive Barker's "The Hellbound Heart" has bled, er... led to this movie franchise

heart of darkness.example_clue: This Joseph Conrad work enlisted as "Apocalypse Now"

10 things i hate about you.example_clue: "How do I loathe thee? Let me count the ways" was a tagline for this high school-set adaptation of "The Taming of the Shrew"

c.s. lewis.example_clue: He wrote "The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe" for Lucy Barfield & dedicated it to her from "your affectionate Godfather"

vietnam.example_clue: Kristin Hannah dedicated "The Women" to "the courageous women who served" here, as well as to all veterans & POW/MIA

(shirley) jackson.example_clue: Stephen King dedicated "Firestarter" "in memory of" this "Haunting of Hill House" author

salinger.example_clue: In "Franny & Zooey", he paid homage to William Shawn of The New Yorker, calling him his closest friend & "lover of the long shot"

joan didion.example_clue: She dedicated "Play It as It Lays" to her husband John Gregor Dunne, & he dedicated "True Confessions" partly to her

chicago.example_clue: The 100th pick for Oprah's Book Club is Ann Napolitano's "Hello Beautiful", set in this city that brought Oprah to fame

mindy kaling.example_clue: For her book club & her publishing imprint, this sitcom star chose a book by Sonali Dev about 3 generations of Indian women

shondaland.example_clue: Fiction & nonfiction mix in the picks from this company, the empire of TV producer Ms. Rimes

dua lipa.example_clue: Granddaughter of a noted Kosovo historian, this British singer founded the Service95 Book Club

spring awakening.example_clue: Library Science from Kaia Gerber & Alyssa Reeder (good name!) has quirky picks like this 1891 play about teens & sex, later a 2006 musical

pippi longstocking.example_clue: astridlindgren.com says this girl has been translated into 80 languages; in French, she's Fifi Brindacier

kerouac.example_clue: When this Beat author died in 1969, 12 years after the publication of his best-known book, his estate was valued at $91

the odyssey.example_clue: Nikos Kazantzakis felt this epic poem needed a 33,333-line sequel in which the hero gets tired of being home & leaves once more

walter scott.example_clue: This Edinburgh author was also a publishing investor & an 1826 bankruptcy left him liable for 120,000 pounds

marlon james.example_clue: This Jamaican author of "A Brief History of Seven Killings" had his first novel, "John Crow's Devil", rejected 78 times

the world book.example_clue: Encyclopedia Britannica says this American rival is for "elementary through high-school students"

green book.example_clue: A Harlem postal worker began compiling this travel guide in 1936

the little red book.example_clue: The pocket-sized "Quotations from Chairman Mao Tse-tung" is also known by this diminutive & colorful name

the book of kells.example_clue: "The Arrest of Christ" is one of the illuminations in this medieval masterpiece held in Dublin's Trinity College Library

the pillow book.example_clue: An 11th century Japanese courtesan wrote the best-known of the diaries called this, traditionally kept in sleeping quarters
